svg.text: Seeed XIAO ESP32-S3 ↔ Waveshare 7.5" e-Paper HAT (B) · 800×480 · Red/Black/White
21×17.5 mm · Built-in LiPo charging via USB-C
D10(GPIO9)=MOSI, D8(GPIO7)=CLK are hardware SPI pins on XIAO ESP32-S3. D1/D2/D3/D4/D5 = GPIO2/3/4/5/6. All signals are 3.3V logic.
GxEPD2 class: GxEPD2_3C<GxEPD2_750c_Z08, GxEPD2_750c_Z08::HEIGHT/4> · BUSY=LOW means busy · D5/PWR must be HIGH before display.init()
Connect to HAT's 9-pin labeled connector. Add 100µF electrolytic + 100nF ceramic bypass caps on VCC–GND near display connector (shown on wire above).
Power — AKZYTUE 3000 mAh LiPo · JST PH2.0mm · Slide Switch → XIAO BAT pads
XIAO ESP32-S3 has built-in LiPo charging via USB-C — no external charge controller needed
Charges via USB-C when plugged in
XIAO onboard JST: GH1.25mm (1.25mm pitch) — incompatible
Fix: solder JST PH2.0mm socket on perfboard,
wire directly to XIAO BAT+ and GND pads (underside)
⚠ Verify polarity with multimeter before connecting

Wiring: battery (+) → slide switch → XIAO BAT+ pad. Battery (−) → XIAO GND. Solder JST PH2.0mm female socket on perfboard; run wires to XIAO underside pads (bypasses onboard GH1.25mm socket, charging IC still active).
Deep sleep current: ~14µA. Active cycle (Wi-Fi + render): ~200mA for ~15s. Estimated runtime: 3000mAh ÷ ~0.85mAh/cycle ≈ 3,500 cycles ≈ 147 days at 60-min refresh.
No TP4056 or boost converter needed — XIAO ESP32-S3 integrates a LiPo charge controller. Recharge by plugging USB-C into the XIAO directly.
